name: Newsdesk (bulletin sources)
description: Normalisation layer between the bulletin board and its news sources. A caller may configure any number of sources of two very different kinds — RSS/Atom feeds (dated, verbatim, free) and plain news pages (undated, read and rewritten by the AI SysOp, needs a key). newsdesk.fetch() funnels every kind into one entry shape {text, title, url, ts, source, kind}; newsdesk.merge() then orders them newest-first and drops duplicates. bulletins.py keeps only the cache, the cadence and the presentation.
guidelines: Every new kind of source gets its own _from_* function that returns the standard entry shape — never special-case a source kind downstream of fetch().
A source that fails must return [] and never take the whole board down: fetch() swallows its exceptions on purpose.
Each source may contribute up to `count` entries; the cut to `count` happens after the merge, so one busy feed cannot crowd out a quiet one.
The bulletin cache is keyed by a signature of the enabled source URLs (bulletins._signature) — changing the mix must invalidate it.
Feed dates are parsed in feeds.timestamp(); a date without a timezone is read as UTC so sources stay comparable.
anti_patterns: Stamping undated entries (AI-read pages) with the fetch time so they can join the timeline — a made-up date silently outranks real ones. They sort after the dated entries instead.
Storing a single 'url' in the bulletins config: it is a list of sources now, and newsdesk.normalize() folds the legacy string form in.
Deduping only by URL — wire stories arrive with different URLs but identical headlines, so _key() falls back to the normalised title.
decisions: Feeds are tried first for every source URL because that path is free; a page is only fetched when the feed parse yields nothing, and a feed advertised in the page's <head> still wins over reading it with the AI.
needs_ai() is true only when EVERY known source is a page — with one feed in the mix a refresh still delivers something and must not be blocked on a missing key.
MAX_SOURCES = 8, since every source is fetched during the dial-in sequence.
